title: 10 best herbal capsules for ovulation pain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for ovulation pain are a natural supplement designed to alleviate the discomfort and cramping associated with ovulation. They help to ease menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms that women often experience during this time. The best herbal capsules for ovulation pain include Cimicifuga racemosa, Paeonia lactiflora, Angelica sinensis, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Vitex agnus-castus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica archangelica, and Actaea racemosa. If left untreated, ovulation pain can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, irritability, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting relationships, work, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain the herb angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation. The capsule's active compounds, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, help to relax uterine muscles and reduce prostaglandin production, which can contribute to pain during ovulation. As a result, angelica capsules may provide natural relief from ovulation pain, allowing women to better manage their menstrual cycles and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic properties: Angelica's spasmolytic compounds, such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, help to relax uterine muscles and reduce cramping associated with ovulation pain, providing relief from discomfort and menstrual cramps.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in angelica, including ferulic acid and apiol, help to reduce inflammation and swelling that can occur during ovulation, which may contribute to pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Angelica's antioxidant compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, help to protect the body from free radical damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate ovulation pain and menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: A sesquiterpene lactone found in Angelica archangelica, ligustilide has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate ovulation pain.
Butylphthalide: A sesquiterpene found in the plant, butylphthalide has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce ovulation pain and discomfort.
Coumarins: A class of phenolic compounds present in Angelica archangelica, coumarins have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help ease ovulation pain and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Leaves: Contain various bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from ovulation-related pain.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, rhyzomes are also rich in ferulic acid and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried angelica archangelica root and stems from a trusted herbal supplier.
Grind the dried angelica archangelica root and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 30g of the fine powder with 70g of tapioca starch in a bowl to create a blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100-200 capsules with 250mg of the powder blend each.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain a unique compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, alleviating the cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's warming properties help to improve blood flow, further reducing tension in the pelvic area and promoting a more comfortable ovulation process. By addressing these physical symptoms, herbal Ceylon cinnamon capsules can provide relief from the pain and discomfort often experienced during ovulation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ovaries and pelvic area, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with ovulation.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon capsules' high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and pain during ovulation, promoting a healthier reproductive environment.
Warm and soothing properties: The warm and soothing nature of cinnamon helps relax the uterine muscles and ease cramping, reducing menstrual discomfort and pain often associated with ovulation.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps alleviate ovulation pain by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and pain associated with ovulation.
Eugenol: Eugenol has a sedative effect on the uterine muscles, which may help reduce cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Cinnamic acid: Cinnamic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ovulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamomum verum leaves are used to make capsules for ovulation pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Cinnamomum verum barks are used to make capsules for ovulation pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum verum rhyzomes are used to make capsules for ovulation pain due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation associated with ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from mature trees under the full moon.
Grind 250g of the harvested bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 50g of tapioca starch in a bowl to form a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the powder blend using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with ovulation pain because they regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen levels. The herb's active compound, vitex agnus-castus, stimulates the release of luteinizing hormone, which triggers ovulation and alleviates symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings associated with PMS. Additionally, chaste tree capsules may also help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, further easing the discomfort experienced during ovulation. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, chaste tree capsules provide natural relief from ovulation-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Aglycone Pseudostellaria: This compound has been shown to regulate the balance of hormones, particularly estrogen and progesterone, which can help alleviate ovulation pain caused by hormonal fluctuations.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in chaste tree capsules can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to stabilize hormone levels and reduce symptoms such as cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chaste tree capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with ovulation by reducing inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with ovulation pain by regulating hormonal balance, particularly reducing levels of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), which contribute to menstrual discomfort.
Acnide: Acnide in Vitex agnus castus capsules has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain associated with ovulation, while also regulating menstrual cycles.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with ovulation, promoting overall menstrual health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part, as they contain a high concentration of iridoid glycosides, which are believed to contribute to the herb's therapeutic effects.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high content of vitexin and other bioactive compounds that may help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate ovulation pain.
Leaves: Leaves are also used, as they contain a combination of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides that may help to balance hormone levels and reduce menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500 milligrams of dried vitex agnus castus root bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin and 100 milligrams of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Add 20 milliliters of glycerin and 20 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and stir until the mixture is smooth.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill empty vegetable capsules with the herbal mixture in about 20 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with ovulation pain because of its rich content of phytoestrogens, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These phytoestrogens help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can cause discomfort and pain during ovulation. Additionally, maidenhair tree's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to alleviate cramping and spasms associated with ovulation. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones and reducing inflammation, maidenhair tree capsules can provide relief from the uncomfortable symptoms of ovulation pain.
remedy_properties: Vitex agnus-castus (Chaste Tree): Contains flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate menstrual cramps.

Angelica sinensis (Chinese Angelica or Dong Quai): Has been traditionally used to ease menstrual pain and other symptoms associated with menstruation, possibly due to its ability to dilate blood vessels and reduce inflammation.
Cinnamomum cassia (Chinese Cinnamon): Contains cinnamic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help relieve cramps and discomfort during ovulation.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce ovulation pain by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis and promoting blood vessel relaxation.

Ginkgolide B: A sesquiterpenoid lactone, ginkgolide B has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects that may help alleviate ovulation pain by reducing blood vessel constriction and promoting smooth muscle relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most widely used part due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which helps in alleviating ovulation pain and promoting menstrual health.

Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds, particularly the kernel, are used to create capsules as they contain high levels of terpene and flavonoid compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and pain associated with ovulation.

Barks: Ginkgo biloba barks are sometimes used to make capsules due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes, which help in soothing and calming the body, reducing pain and discomfort during ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in early fall, ideally when leaves start to turn yellow.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours, monitoring temperature and humidity levels.
Grind 150 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder, avoiding overheating or generating static electricity.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 5 grams of starch or cellulose using a mortar and pestle, ensuring uniform blending.
Fill 100 capsules with the powdered ginkgo biloba mixture using a manual capsule filler, following the manufacturer's recommended dosage of 120 to 240 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pelvic cramps associated with ovulation. Curcumin also helps to relax uterine muscles and improve blood flow, which can ease the discomfort and bloating often experienced during this time. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall reproductive health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce ovulation pain by decreasing inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area.

Curcumin's antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate ovulation pain and other menstrual symptoms.
Turmeric's uterine relaxant properties: Turmeric has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the uterus, which can help alleviate cramps, spasms, and discomfort associated with ovulation pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with ovulation, thereby providing relief from ovulation pain.

Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with ovulation, making it a key contributor to the pain-relieving effects of Curcuma longa.

Demethoxycurcumin: Demethoxycurcumin, a curcuminoid, has shown significant anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with ovulation, making it an important constituent of Curcuma longa capsules for ovulation pain relief.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Most commonly used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause pain and discomfort during ovulation.
Stems: Sometimes used for their potential to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps associated with ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried turmeric roots and sift them to remove lumps and debris.
Weigh 30g of the sifted turmeric powder and mix it with 60g of gelatin powder.
Add 20ml of glycerin to the mixture and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain the herb Angelica sinensis, which has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort. The capsules help to regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating the spasms and contractions that cause ovulation pain. By reducing tension and promoting a sense of calm, dong quai capsules can make the ovulation process more comfortable and manageable for women experiencing this common symptom.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with ovulation, making it a natural remedy for alleviating cramps and pelvic pain.
Antispasmodic properties: The herb's antispasmodic compounds can relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that can cause sharp, stabbing pains during ovulation.

Blood circulation-enhancing properties: Dong quai's ability to improve blood flow can help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce pain associated with ovulation by promoting healthy blood circulation to the pelvic area.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: These compounds help with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's prostaglandin levels, which are involved in pain regulation.
Phenolic acids (specifically Ferulic acid): These acids have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to reducing pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Ligustilide (a sesquiterpene lactone): This constituent helps with ovulation pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are known to cause pain and inflammation during ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are used due to their high content of ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Angelica sinensis are used due to their bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid and ligustilide, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with ovulation.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are used due to their high content of essential oils, including ligustilide, which helps to relax the uterine muscles and reduce pain associated with ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Dry the angelica sinensis roots and stems in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis roots and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a small amount of tapioca starch to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the uniform blend using a powder filling machine.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain a unique combination of herbs that help to regulate the menstrual cycle and ease uterine cramping. The capsules' active ingredients, including Chinese peony root and licorice root, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause cramps and discomfort during ovulation. Additionally, the capsules may also help to increase blood flow to the pelvic area, further reducing pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules contain compounds like paeoniflorin, which can help relax uterine muscles and reduce cramping associated with ovulation pain.
Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' ingredients, including alkaloids and glycosides, may help reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which can contribute to ovulation pain.

Oxytocin regulation property: Paeoniflorin and other compounds in Herbal Chinese Peony Capsules may also regulate oxytocin levels, which can help manage uterine contractions and alleviate ovulation cramps.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with ovulation pain by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Paeoniflorin: This isoflavonoid helps with ovulation pain by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can reduce pain and swelling in the ovaries and surrounding areas.
Benzenoids (such as benzaldehyde): These organic compounds help with ovulation pain by exerting a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to ovulation pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules due to their rich content of paeonol and other compounds that help to alleviate menstrual cramps and pain.
Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of paeonol and other bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules due to their content of glycosides, paeonol, and other compounds that have a soothing effect on the uterus and help to reduce ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora root and flowers for each batch of capsules.
Dry the harvested root and flower mixture in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried mixture into a fine powder using a spice grinder at 2000 rpm for 30 seconds.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 mg of the powdered herbal mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper capsules helps with ovulation pain because it contains a unique compound that has been traditionally used to ease menstrual cramps, bloating, and discomfort. The active ingredient in monk's pepper, piper longum, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions leading to ovulation pain. By reducing these contractions, monk's pepper capsules can provide relief from the physical symptoms associated with ovulation, allowing women to feel more comfortable and relaxed during this time.
remedy_properties: Triterpenes: Monk's pepper contains triterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain associated with ovulation.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in monk's pepper capsules have been shown to have a positive effect on the uterus, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate cramping and discomfort during ovulation.

Phenolic acids: Monk's pepper also contains phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to the alleviation of ovulation pain.
remedy_constituents: Osthole: Osthole is a coumarin derivative that helps with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles, thereby alleviating cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Cnidii Rhizoma polysaccharides: Cnidii Rhizoma polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with ovulation.

Flavonoids (including luteolin and isoorientin): Flavonoids in Cnidium monnieri have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to reduce the severity of ovulation pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the primary part used in Cnidium monnieri supplements due to their high concentration of coumarin and other bioactive compounds.
Seeds: Seeds are another commonly used part, as they contain a high amount of essential oils, including ligustilide, which may help alleviate menstrual cramps.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used in Cnidium monnieri supplements, possibly due to their reported anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cnidium monnieri roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested cnidium monnieri roots and leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried cnidium monnieri roots and 15 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder.
Mix 1000 milliliters of vegetable oil with 300 grams of the powder and heat the mixture for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300 milligrams of the herbal extract and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which is often associated with ovulation pain. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to relax the uterine muscles, easing cramping and discomfort caused by ovulation. As ovulation approaches, the body naturally produces prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate contractions of the uterus. Licorice capsules help to balance these prostaglandins, resulting in less severe pain during ovulation.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a saponin found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce ovulation pain by decreasing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in licorice root have antioxidant and anti-spasmodic effects that help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation by reducing oxidative stress and muscle contractions.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin, a flavonoid found in licorice root, has been shown to inhibit the release of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and can contribute to ovulation pain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ovulation.
Flavonoids: Specifically, quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help mitigate pain and discomfort caused by ovulation.

Glycyrrhizin: An active triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-androgenic properties, which can help reduce prostaglandin levels, decrease inflammation, and alleviate pain during ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for ovulation pain relief due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for ovulation pain relief due to their ability to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for ovulation pain relief due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a length of 30-60 cm at the right time of year.
Crush the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5-10 minutes.
Mix 3-5 grams of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 5-10 grams of a natural filler like cornstarch.
Use a capsule filler to fill 100-200 empty capsules with the mixture for 10-15 minutes.
Store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain compounds that stimulate uterine contractions, helping to ease cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the ovaries and Fallopian tubes, further alleviating pelvic pressure and pain. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic effects help eliminate excess fluid retention, which can contribute to bloating and discomfort during ovulation. By addressing these underlying causes of ovulation pain, herbal dandelion capsules provide natural relief for women experiencing cramps and discomfort during this crucial phase of their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, may help reduce inflammation in the pelvic area and ease ovulation-related discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and pain during ovulation.

Pain-relieving properties: Dandelion's alkaloid compounds, such as taraxasterol, may help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause uterine contractions.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain associated with ovulation.
Terpenoids: Specifically, terpenoids like taraxasterol in Dandelion have shown anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which may help reduce ovulation pain.
Polysaccharides: The soluble fiber and prebiotic properties of Dandelion's polysaccharides may contribute to reduced inflammation and pain by promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate ovulation pain.
Roots: The roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and may help reduce ovulation pain.

Flowers: The flowers are used due to their content of flavonoids and other compounds that have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried taraxacum officinale root and leaf material on a sunny day after morning dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 100g of the dried taraxacum officinale material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5g of the powder with 95g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with 250mg of the taraxacum officinale powder mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules help with ovulation pain because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to modulate prostaglandins and reduce inflammation. This anti-inflammatory effect can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation. Additionally, St John's Wort has been found to have a natural relaxant property, which can calm the uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from painful menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: A natural antidepressant that reduces inflammation and modulates the body's response to stress, which can contribute to reduced ovulation pain.
Hypericin: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce prostaglandins (hormone-like substances) that cause cramping and discomfort during ovulation.

Flavonoids and Phenolic Acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which may help relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms that occur during ovulation, leading to less pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hypericin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation pain.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids, amino acids, and glycosides that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of ovulation-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum in sufficient quantity for desired number of capsules.
Measure out 2 grams of dried st john's wort per capsule using a digital scale accurately.
Grind the measured dried st john's wort into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle carefully.
Mix the powdered st john's wort with 50 milliliters of coconut oil or carrier oil in a small bowl thoroughly.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal tightly for immediate use storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain flavonoids, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation. The capsules also possess phytoestrogens, which can help regulate hormonal balance and ease the transition to ovulation. Additionally, hawthorn's vasodilatory effects may help improve blood flow and reduce spasms in the uterine lining, further contributing to a more comfortable ovulation process. This natural approach can provide relief from painful ovulation without relying on pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Crataegus monogyna extract: Hawthorn's flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and anthraquinones have been shown to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with ovulation pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's polyphenols and saponins have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by ovulation pain.

Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds (e.g., flavonoids, phenolic acids) can protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by ovulation-related inflammation, further alleviating symptoms of ovulation pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, which help alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Buds: Containing bioactive compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, these buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and inflammation during ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and stems according to traditional herbalist guidelines for a minimum of two weeks.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccation at room temperature for three to four days.
Grind one part dried crataegus monogyna plant material into fine powder using a mortar and pestle and add two parts dried ginger root powder as a carrier agent.
Mix 400 milligrams of the powdered blend with 100 milliliters of pure vegetable oil and let it infuse for six hours at room temperature under gentle agitation.

Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass containers and fill capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal extract according to individual needs and preparation time within three days of opening.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain salvia miltiorrhiza, a traditional Chinese herb that has been used for centuries to treat various gynecological issues. The capsules work by reducing inflammation and relieving cramps caused by ovulation. They also help regulate the menstrual cycle, which can alleviate discomfort and promote smoother ovulation. As a result, women who take Danshen capsules may experience fewer symptoms of ovulation pain, allowing them to feel more comfortable and at ease during this time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Danshen capsules contain compounds like salvianolic acid A, which helps reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with ovulation cramps.
Antispasmodic properties: The herb's bioactive compounds, such as tanshinone IIA, can relax uterine muscles and alleviate spasms that may contribute to painful menstrual cramps.

Oxytocin-releasing and relaxing effects: Danshen capsules may help stimulate the release of oxytocin, a hormone that helps regulate contractions and relaxation of the uterus, which could reduce menstrual pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the danshen plant are used to make medicinal capsules as they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, such as tanshinones and phenolic acids, which help alleviate menstrual cramps and relieve ovulation pain.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the danshen plant, are rich in active ingredients that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective for treating ovulation-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia miltiorrhiza root dried extract of 20% tanshinone and grind it into fine powder.
Mix the powdered extract with lactose or starch as a filler to create a uniform blend.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with the herbal blend in doses of 250mg each and seal them tightly.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months preparation time.
Recommend patients take two capsules twice daily before meals with water for a minimum treatment period of three cycles duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White turmeric capsules helps with ovulation pain because they contain curcumin, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin works to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances produced during ovulation that can cause cramping and discomfort. By decreasing prostaglandin production, white turmeric capsules help alleviate the severity of ovulation pain, making it easier for women to manage this natural part of their menstrual cycle. Additionally, curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce bloating and breast tenderness associated with ovulation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma zedoaria rhizomes in the morning after the dew has dried off.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly under running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the washed rhizomes for 7-10 days using a food dehydrator at 60°c or air drying.
Grind the dried curcuma zedoaria into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sifter to a particle size of 100 mesh.
Fill empty capsules with 400mg of the powdered curcuma zedoaria and seal tightly.

remedy_recipe: Dry fresh peppermint leaves in a low-temperature oven for one hour or until crisp.
Combine 10 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 50 milliliters of cold water to create a decoction.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes before filtering and discarding solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with 250 milliliters of glycerin and 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal extract and seal according to manufacturer's instructions.

Mix 250mg of the ground foeniculum vulgare with 500mg of gelatin capsules and fill them by hand within 1 hour.
